data.title: Scammers demand crypto ‘clearance’ fees from ships near Strait of Hormuz: report
data.body: Greek maritime risk firm MARISKS warned shippers of fraudulent Hormuz transit demands paid in bitcoin or USDT, according to Reuters.

data.title: Japan’s JSCC partners with Mizuho, Nomura to test government bonds on blockchain
data.body: The proof-of-concept trial will test the onchain transfer and management of Japanese government bonds on Canton Network.

data.title: Bank of Korea’s new chief vows to push CBDC, deposit tokens; leaves out stablecoins
data.body: BOK Governor Shin Hyun-song previously took a negative stance on stablecoins during his tenure at the BIS.

data.title: Arbitrum freezes $71 million worth of ETH stolen in Kelp DAO exploit
data.body: The Arbitrum Security Council said the frozen funds will only be moved by further action through Arbitrum governance.

data.title: Kelp DAO shifts blame to LayerZero for $292 million exploit; Aave examines bad debt scenarios
data.body: Kelp DAO has countered LayerZero's earlier statement, saying the 1-of-1 DVN setup under criticism was shipped as the default at LayerZero.

data.title: Senate crypto bill faces April setback amid growing pressure to get legislation through: report
data.body: Tensions are building to schedule a hearing to advance crypto market structure legislation, which could reportedly be pushed to May.

data.title: Pokémon TCG marketplaces near record as tokenized collectibles show staying power
data.body: The tokenization layer of these platforms is being used as a liquidity wrapper, as opposed to a purely speculative venue.

data.title: Inside Bitwise’s Avalanche ETP and institutional strategy with Matt Hougan
data.body: Matt Hougan explains how ETFs, tokenization, and institutional capital are reshaping crypto and driving the next phase of growth.

data.title: DeFi losses top $600 million in weeks as Kelp DAO exploit drags TVL to one-year low
data.body: Kelp Dao, Aave and LayerZero are pointing the finger at each other and it is still unclear how losses will be distributed.

data.title: Grayscale’s amended Hyperliquid ETF filing drops Coinbase as custodian, names Anchorage
data.body: Grayscale previously tapped Anchorage as a secondary custodian for a portion of the firm’s Bitcoin and Ethereum trusts’ holdings.

data.title: A year under Paul Atkins: SEC crypto embrace grows as prediction markets draw scrutiny
data.body: About a year into the job, SEC Chair Paul Atkins says he is on a mission to "advance, clarify and transform" or (ACT).

data.title: Coinbase-incubated x402 protocol unveils app-store for AI bots
data.body: X402 creator Erik Reppel noted agentic commerce is already reshaping activation costs with bots accessing services on a per-use basis.

data.title: Tether joins $8 million strategic funding round in RWA firm Kaio
data.body: "Kaio’s unique position unlocks new pathways for capital formation and investment," Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino said.

data.title: Tether discloses nearly 2 million-share stake in Bitmain-linked Antalpha
data.body: Nasdaq-listed Antalpha’s lending business is growing quickly despite a more than 27% drop in its stock price.

data.title: Ripple races to make XRP Ledger quantum-ready as computing threat turns ‘credible’
data.body: The company also made the case that XRP Ledger is better prepared than other blockchains, including Ethereum.

data.title: Tom Lee says ‘crypto winter is much closer to ending’ as Bitmine buys another 101,627 ETH
data.body: Bitmine bought 101,627 ETH in a week, pushing its treasury to 4.12% of ether's supply as chairman Lee calls crypto winter's end.

data.title: UK gas firm downplays bitcoin mining plans after report suggested pivot
data.body: The firm said it is exploring the potential to deploy bitcoin mining, but remains committed to progressing the site for UK energy security.

data.title: Bitcoin holds ‘fragile equilibrium’ near $75K as ETF demand cushions crypto market despite Hormuz tensions: analysts
data.body: Nearly $1 billion in spot bitcoin ETF inflows and rising stablecoin liquidity suggest institutional demand is still stemming pullbacks.

data.title: BIS says stablecoins act more like ETFs than money, warns of fragmentation without global rules: report
data.body: The BIS warned stablecoins resemble ETFs, not money, and called for global rules to prevent fragmentation across the $300 billion market.
